stories.title: World Cup 2026 Expands to 48 Teams with New Knockout Format
stories.desc: The FIFA World Cup expands to 48 teams for the first time, featuring 12 groups with 104 total matches across a 39-day tournament from June 11 to July 19, 2026.

stories.title: Teams Must Submit Preliminary Squads by May 11
stories.desc: FIFA requires teams to submit preliminary lists of 35-55 players by May 11, with final 23-26 player rosters due between May 25 and June 1 ahead of the tournament kickoff.

stories.title: Panini Launches Record 980-Sticker World Cup Album
stories.desc: Panini officially launched its largest-ever FIFA World Cup sticker album on April 28 at Wembley Stadium, featuring 980 stickers including 68 special editions across 112 pages.

stories.title: 14 Panini Stickers Only Available Outside Standard Packs
stories.desc: The 2026 Panini album includes 14 exclusive stickers unavailable in standard packs, with 12 hidden inside specially marked Coca-Cola bottles starting mid-May.

stories.title: Four Nations Make World Cup Debut in 2026
stories.desc: Cape Verde, Curacao, Jordan, and Uzbekistan will make their FIFA World Cup debuts, while DR Congo and Haiti return after 52 years since their last appearance in 1974.

stories.title: Mexico to Host Opening Match vs South Africa on June 11
stories.desc: Mexico opens the 2026 World Cup on June 11 at Estadio Azteca in Mexico City against South Africa, with USA facing Paraguay in Los Angeles the following day.
